properties.label: Bucket Name (*)
properties.tooltip: The name of the bucket to create. General purpose buckets - For information about bucket naming restrictions, see Bucket naming rules in the Amazon S3 User Guide. Directory buckets - When you use this operation with a directory bucket, you must use path-style requests in the format https://s3express-control.region_code.amazonaws.com/bucket-name . Virtual-hosted-style requests aren't supported. Directory bucket names must be unique in the chosen Availability Zone. Bucket names must also follow the format bucket_base_name--az_id--x-s3 (for example, DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET--usw2-az1--x-s3). For information about bucket naming restrictions, see Directory bucket naming rules in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

properties.label: Region
properties.tooltip: Select the region in which you want to create a bucket.

properties.label: Canned ACL
properties.tooltip: The canned ACL to apply to the bucket.

properties.content: ## Choose a way to create S3 bucket

jsonSchema.title: GitLab configuration to raise pull request for terrafrom file

ui:options.tooltip: Branch name or commit SHA to create the branch from.
The path of the file to create.
The ID or path of the project.

properties.label: Role Name (*)
properties.tooltip: The name of the role to create. IAM user, group, role, and policy names must be unique within the account. Names are not distinguished by case. For example, you cannot create resources named both "MyResource" and "myresource". This parameter allows (through its regex pattern) a string of characters consisting of upper and lowercase alphanumeric characters with no spaces. You can also include any of the following characters: _+=,.@-.

properties.label: Path
properties.tooltip: The path to the role. For more information about paths, see IAM Identifiers in the IAM Role Guide. This parameter is optional. If it is not included, it defaults to a slash (/). This parameter allows (through its regex pattern) a string of characters consisting of either a forward slash (/) by itself or a string that must begin and end with forward slashes. In addition, it can contain any ASCII character from the ! through the DEL character, including most punctuation characters, digits, and upper and lowercased letters.

properties.label: Description
properties.placeholder: e.g., This role provides you admin access
properties.tooltip: A description of the role.

properties.label: Max Session Duration
properties.tooltip: The maximum session duration (in seconds) that you want to set for the specified role. If you do not specify a value for this setting, the default value of one hour is applied. This setting can have a value from 1 hour to 12 hours. Anyone who assumes the role from the CLI or API can use the DurationSeconds API parameter or the duration-seconds CLI parameter to request a longer session. The MaxSessionDuration setting determines the maximum duration that can be requested using the DurationSeconds parameter. If users don't specify a value for the DurationSeconds parameter, their security credentials are valid for one hour by default. This applies when you use the AssumeRole* API operations or the assume-role* CLI operations but does not apply when you use those operations to create a console URL. For more information, see Using IAM roles in the IAM User Guide.

properties.label: Assume Role Policy Document (*)
properties.tooltip: The trust relationship policy document that grants an entity permission to assume the role. In IAM, you must provide a JSON policy that has been converted to a string. However, for CloudFormation templates formatted in YAML, you can provide the policy in JSON or YAML format. CloudFormation always converts a YAML policy to JSON format before submitting it to IAM. The regex pattern used to validate this parameter is a string of characters consisting of the following: Any printable ASCII character ranging from the space character through the end of the ASCII character range The printable characters in the Basic Latin and Latin-1 Supplement character set. The special characters tab, line feed, and carriage return Upon success, the response includes the same trust policy in JSON format.

properties.content:
Please avoid using the policy template shared above.  
Use the policy generator link given to generate policy [Policy generator](https://awspolicygen.s3.amazonaws.com/policygen.html)

ui:options.tooltip: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM policy you want to attach. For more information about ARNs, see Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) in the Amazon Web Services General Reference.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM policy you want to detach. For more information about ARNs, see Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) in the Amazon Web Services General Reference.

properties.label: Policy Name (*)
properties.tooltip:
The friendly name of the policy.
IAM user, group, role, and policy names must be unique within the account. 
Names are not distinguished by case. For example, you cannot create resources named both “MyResource” and “myresource”.

properties.label: Description
properties.placeholder: e.g., This policy provide you admin access
properties.tooltip:
A friendly description of the policy.
Typically used to store information about the permissions defined in the policy.

properties.label: Path
properties.tooltip: The path for the policy. For more information about paths, see IAM Identifiers in the IAM Policy Guide. This parameter is optional. If it is not included, it defaults to a slash (/). This parameter allows (through its regex pattern) a string of characters consisting of either a forward slash (/) by itself or a string that must begin and end with forward slashes. In addition, it can contain any ASCII character from the ! through the DEL character, including most punctuation characters, digits, and upper and lowercased letters.

properties.label: Policy Document (*)
properties.tooltip: The JSON policy document that you want to use as the content for the new policy.

properties.content:
Please avoid using the policy template shared above.  
Use the policy generator link given to generate policy [Policy generator](https://awspolicygen.s3.amazonaws.com/policygen.html)

> ⚠️ **Note:** Tags must be provided as a JSON object with key value pairs and array objects are not allowed in Key values. The example for the same is given below:  
> ```json
> {"Environment": "Production","Role":"Developer"}
> ```

ui:options.placeholder: e.g., The security group helps you maintain the firewall
ui:options.tooltip: A description of the security group. This is for informational purposes only.

The name of the security group.
Constraints: Up to 255 characters in length. Cannot start with sg-.
Constraints for EC2-Classic: ASCII characters Constraints for EC2-VPC: a-z, A-Z, 0-9, spaces, and ._-:/()#,@[]+=&;{}!$*.
Region in which you want to create security group.

properties.content: For details on IP permission structure, refer to the [AWS IP Permission documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_IpPermission.html).
For details on IP permission structure, refer to the [AWS IP Permission documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_IpPermission.html).
For details on IP permission structure, refer to the [AWS IP Permission documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_IpPermission.html).
For details on IP permission structure, refer to the [AWS IP Permission documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_IpPermission.html).

> ⚠️ **Note:** Tags must be provided as a JSON array like:  
> ```json
> [{"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"}]
> ```
> 🔒 **Important:** Keys must be named **"Key"** and **"Value"** (case-sensitive). Do **not** rename them.
⚠️ Note: Please attach only one file at a time, multiple file attachments are not supported.

properties.content:
I made this fancy app with a bunch of Datadog Actions. It includes most of the services critical to the TechStories architecture. I hope I didn't forget anything! 

- XOXO Karl

properties.label: Type
properties.tooltip: Choose the queue type for your application or cloud infrastructure.

properties.label: Name (*)
properties.tooltip: The name of the new queue. The following limits apply to this name: A queue name can have up to 80 characters. Valid values: alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), and underscores (_). A FIFO queue name must end with the .fifo suffix. Queue URLs and names are case-sensitive.

properties.label: Region
properties.tooltip: Region in which you want to create a queue.

properties.label: Visibility timeout (In seconds)
properties.tooltip: The visibility timeout for the queue, in seconds. Valid values: An integer from 0 to 43,200. Default: 30.

properties.label: Message retention period (In seconds)
properties.placeholder: A value from 60 seconds (1 minute) to 1,209,600 seconds (14 days). Default: 345,600 (4 days)
properties.tooltip: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS retains a message. Valid values: An integer from 60 seconds (1 minute) to 1,209,600 seconds (14 days). Default: 345,600 (4 days). When you change a queue’s attributes, the change can take up to 60 seconds for most of the attributes to propagate throughout the Amazon SQS system. Changes made to the MessageRetentionPeriod attribute can take up to 15 minutes and will impact existing messages in the queue potentially causing them to be expired and deleted if the MessageRetentionPeriod is reduced below the age of existing messages.

properties.label: Delivery delay (In seconds)
properties.placeholder: A value between 0 to 900 seconds (15 minutes). Default: 0
properties.tooltip: The length of time, in seconds, for which the delivery of all messages in the queue is delayed. Valid values: An integer from 0 to 900 seconds (15 minutes). Default: 0.

properties.label: Maximum message size (KiB)
properties.placeholder: A value between 1 to 256 KiB. Default: 256
properties.tooltip: The limit of how many Kibibytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it. Valid values: An integer from 1 KiB to 256 KiB. Default: 256 KiB.

properties.label: Receive message wait time (In seconds)
properties.tooltip: The length of time, in seconds, for which a ReceiveMessage action waits for a message to arrive. Valid values: An integer from 0 to 20 (seconds). Default: 0.

properties.label: Server-side encryption
properties.tooltip: Amazon SQS provides in-transit encryption by default. To add at-rest encryption to your queue, enable server-side encryption.

properties.label: Encryption key type
Data key reuse period (In Seconds)
properties.placeholder: An value  between 60 seconds and 86,400 seconds
properties.tooltip: An integer representing seconds, between 60 seconds (1 minute) and 86,400 seconds (24 hours). Default: 300 (5 minutes). A shorter time period provides better security but results in more calls to KMS which might incur charges after Free Tier.

properties.label: Custom master key (Required if SSE-KMS is selected)
properties.tooltip: The ID of an Amazon Web Services managed customer master key (CMK) for Amazon SQS or a custom CMK.

properties.label: JSON Access Policy (Required)
properties.tooltip: The queue’s policy. A valid Amazon Web Services policy.

properties.content:
Please avoid using the policy template shared above.  
Use the policy generator link given to generate policy [Policy generator](https://awspolicygen.s3.amazonaws.com/policygen.html)

properties.label: Select which source queues can use this queue as the dead-letter queue
Select the ARN for each source queue (10 at max). Used only when redrive permission is set to By Queue
Set this queue to receive undeliverable messages
properties.tooltip: Send undeliverable messages to a dead-letter queue.

properties.label: Choose queues (*)
properties.tooltip: The queue acting as DLQ.

properties.label: Maximum receives (*)
properties.placeholder: The value should lie between 1-1000. Default: 10
properties.tooltip: The number of times a message is delivered to the source queue before being moved to the dead-letter queue. Default: 10. The value should lie between 1-1000. When the ReceiveCount for a message exceeds the maxReceiveCount for a queue, Amazon SQS moves the message to the dead-letter-queue.

properties.label: Deduplication scope
FIFO throughput limit

properties.content: Central hub for operating EC2 instances and configuring security groups.
Create, manage, and operate SQS queues and DLQs with ease.
Manage the identity lifecycle — IAM roles and custom access policies.
Life is easy when you can click a button - Karl, 2025

name: TechStories Service Overview
